articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup

commentary.text: Ghana fans celebrate on the streets of Johannesburg after beating USA.

Ghana are the sole African representatives left in the World Cup, but Firdoose Moonda believes South African support for the Black Stars is only superficial. Meanwhile, Uruguay will be without influential defender Diego Godin for the match.
Join us at 1830 GMT on Friday to see whether Ghana can become the first ever African nation to reach the World Cup semi-finals.

After the fireworks in Port Elizabeth earlier today, lets hope the second quarter-final of South Africa 2010 can live up to its billing. Uruguay face the team with a continent in their corner, Milovan Rajevac's Ghana.
Ghana include Sulley Muntari in the starting eleven for the first time in the tournament, with Isaac Vorsah returning to the starting line up at the expense of Jonathan Mensah.
Uruguay start with Mauricio Victorino in place of Diego Godin, Alvaro Fernandez also comes in for Alvaro Pereira.

The question will be whether or not Ghana can handle the pressure of being Africa's only remaining team. If they win today, they'll be the first African nation to reach the semi-finals of a World Cup.
This match represents an excellent chance for Uruguay. Blessed with extremely talented attacking players, the prospect of a semi-final with Netherlands will be one they'll relish.
In Diego Forlan and Luis Suarez, Uruguay have two of the tournament's most lethal front men. Both will still have designs on claiming the golden boot, and will be eager to find the net tonight.
Another man in a rich vien of form is Ghana's Asamoah Gyan. The striker, who wears the number 3, has been a talismanic figure for the Black Stars so far in this World Cup.
The teams are making their way out onto the pitch, the time is almost upon us.
Stat Attack: Ghana's Asamoah Gyan tops the list for most shots at the tournament with Argentina's Lionel Messi on 23.
Stat Attack: Uruguay have made more defensive clearances than any other team, 58, and have completed the second highest amount, 19.
KICK OFF Ghana get the match started.
First foul of the game, Mensah going through the back of Luis Suarez.
Uruguay looking to keep the ball and settle into a rhythm. First offside decision goes against Luis Suarez.
Forlan makes an excellent burst through the middle, his through ball forces Fernandez slightly too wide and his cross is a poor one.
First corner to Uruguay, Vorsah dispossessing Suarez.
Forlan's low corner was creeping in at the near post before Inkoom hoofs it behind.
Mensah with a solid headed clearance from the second corner, Arevalo volleys well wide.

From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Soccer City appears to be behind the Ghana team - there have been plenty of public calls for local backing. But there was definitely some excitement during that burst from Diego Forlan.
First chance for Ghana, a free kick out wide for a foul on Gyan.
A poor delivery, the chances is wasted and it's a Uruguay goal kick.
Cavani launches a long-range pass towards Suarez on the far side, just too long and it's a Ghana goal kick.

Poor from Inkoom, losing possession in the midfield and Luis Suarez is released. Suarez forces his way past Vorsah before curling a right-footed effort at Richard Kingson, who just manages to keep hold of the ball.
Foul by Muntari on Fernandez, another chance to get the ball into the danger area...
Forlan's free kick finds Lugano alone at the far post. His cushioned header falls for Arevalo, who floats the ball harmlessly into Kingson.
Kingson gets lucky! Forlan's powerful free kick is straight at the goalkeeper. He can't hold it however, and is fortunate to hear the referee's whistle after tustling with Luis Suarez.
Uruguay starting to put the pressure on. They have another corner...
The whistle blows for a push by Alvaro Fernandez, a wasted opportunity for Uruguay.
Forlan turns and runs at Ghana again, they are starting to look a bit edgy. Mensah clears for another corner to Uruguay.
A smart save from Kingson! Cavani's flick on deflects off Mensah, and fortunately for Ghana straight at Kingson.
Forlan goes on a run.
Fucile booked for a foul on Asamoah. He won't play in the semi-final. Dangerous free kick for Ghana.
A fantastic delivery from Muntari, Mensah and Vorsah look to get on the end of it, but Fernandez heads clear.
Comment from John Brewin: Kingson belied his status as Wigan third-choice with an odd save. And that looked a very harsh yellow card.
Suarez brought down by Vorsah, there have been a lot of set pieces in this game so far. Forlan over the dead ball again...
Forlan's ball is over hit and runs out for a goal kick.
Kevin-Prince Boateng fights for the ball with Uruguay midfielder Diego Perez.
First shot at goal for Diego Forlan. Cavani slips the Atletico man in. He exchanges passes with Arevalo before bending an effort high and wide. A warning for Ghana.
Kingson saves again from Suarez! Ghana switch off as Suarez is played in straight from a throw in. Suarez turns, his vicious strike is excellently saved.
Diego Lugano is limping for Uruguay, a big worry.
Yet another Uruguay corner. I've already lost count of how many there have been.
The corner is taken short and is eventually headed clear by Vorsah. Ghana are defending so many set pieces.
ESPN Stats & Info: All three of Diego Forlan's World Cup goals have come against African nations.
Ghana earn a corner after a poor corner from Victorino. So many corners!!!
So close for Ghana! Vorsah climbs excellently above Lugano, his powerful header is inches wide. First sight of goal for Ghana.

Ghana have come to life! A fantastic run from Boateng, he cuts inside and finds Gyan. The striker guides the ball towards goal, only to see it end up just the wrong side of the post. Far better from the Black Stars.
From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Vorsah's header and Gyan's miss had the crowd in near raptures. One feels that Uruguay need to score soon or else they will be caught by the Ghanaians.
Uruguay seem to have weathered the mini Ghana storm. They need to keep the ball for a while.
Clever ball from Boateng! He clips the ball towards Gyan with the outside of his left foot, only slightly too long and Muslera can gather.
The flags up against Diego Forlan, Ghana are looking dangerous now.
Richard Kingson makes a save.
Scotti on for Lugano
A huge blow for Uruguay. Their skipper forced to withdraw. How will this impact on them?
Immediately Ghana have a chance. A deep ball to the back post finds Muntari. With Musleri rooted to the spot, Muntari can only guide the ball wide.
Suarez getting fouled by Suarez again, he's coming in for some rough treatment.
Forlan over another set piece.
Awful. Absolutely awful. Forlan tries to play Fernandez in down the side of the wall, his ball is over hit and it runs out for a goal kick.
Fucile is down and this doesn't look good.
Fucile collides with Inkoom in mid air and lands on his head. He's out cold.
Fortunately Fucile is back on his feet, that could've been very nasty.
A snapshot from Gyan, trying to catch Muslera off his line from 40 yards out. It's comfortably saved in the end.
Isaac Vorsah sends a header just wide
Inkoom gets to the byline a floats an delicate ball into the box. Boateng attempts an audacious overhead kick, which is sliced over the bar.
TWO MINUTES ADDED TIME
GOAL Sulley Muntari fires Ghana into the lead! Muntari has the ball 35 yards from goal. Nothing seems to be on for him, so he unleashes a fierce low drive that skips past Muslera! What a goal!
HALF TIME Muntari's wonder goal is the difference between the sides at the break.

From John Brewin in Johannesburg: An unbelievable sound there as Muntari scored. The keeper was lost. Uruguay pay for not taking their earlier chances. Ghana are in a half-time huddle and are imploring each other on.
As this game has progressed Ghana have got better and better. They will be full of confidence ahead of the second half. Can Uruguay recover from this blow?
Find out Ernesto Garrido's thoughts on how Dunga's failings rubbed off on his team against the Dutch.
Find out Ernst Bouwes reflects on an unexpected victory and a proud day for the Netherlands.
Uruguay are behind for the first time in the tournament. How will they respond after the interval?
Fernando Muslera fails to save Muntari's shot.
KICK OFF Uruguay start the second half in this Johannesburg.
Lodeiro on for Fernandez
Can Lodeiro provide the spark Uruguay need to get back in this game?
ESPN Stats & Info: Muntari's goal was 39 yards out, second longest of this World cup. David Villa had the longest at 40 yards.
Massive shout for a Uruguay penalty! Cavani knocks the ball past Vorsah, and the Ghana defender seems to knock him over. I've seen them given...
Arevalo booked for a challenge on Boateng. Muntari is over the free kick...
The wall does its job, although it seemed to hit Lodeiro's arm.
Muntari celebrates his goal
ESPN Stats & Info: Uruguay have completed just 38.7% of passes in the final third, well below the tournament average of 63%.
Suarez is brought down my Mensah, Uruguay with a free kick just inside the Ghana half.
The game is yet to settle down since the restart. Both teams need to calm down a little.
First corner of the second half, Suarez is in behind a Vorsah is forced to slide the ball behind.
What a waste from Boateng! Gyan heads clear the corner and Boateng streaks clear. Ghana have four against three, but Boateng cannot find a team-mate.
Paintsil the book for a challenge on Fucile.
Forlan once again over a dead ball...
GOAL Forlan with the equaliser! A glorious free kick! bent over the wall, dipping and swerving. Kingson will be disappointed with himself there.
Suarez fouls Mensah in the Ghana penalty area. Have Ghana got what it takes to come back here?
From John Brewin in Johannesburg: A truly wondrous strike from Forlan. Kingson could not work out which way it was going and guessed wrong. That's silenced the crowd.

Decent stuff from Ghana. Boateng feeds the ball to Gyan on the inside left. Gyan bends a low effort straight at Muslera, he spills the ball, Muntari tries to pounce but the ball is cleared for a corner.
Perez carded for hacking down Asamoah. Nasty.
The game is being played at a hectic pace! Really entertaining game at Soccer City!
ESPN Stats & Info: Updated Stat: All four of Diego Forlan's World Cup goals have come against African sides
A moment to forget for Kingson. He allows a tame backpass under his feet and out of play. Yet another Uruguay corner...
Foul given as Suarez tugs down Sarpei. Uruguay attack again and Cavani's cross is into the arms of Kingson.
Suarez with a golden chance! Forlan plucks the ball out of the air fantastically. He lifts an excellent ball to Suarez at the back post, who can only guide the ball wide. Nearly for Uruguay.
Forland with a lovely ball to Lodeiro. The young playmaker slips the ball through to Suarez, who is offside. Uruguay look threatening again.
Muslera punches a Gyan cross off the head of Boateng. Ghana come again, Boateng drives towards goal and seems to be brought down by Scotti. The referee says no penalty...
Gyan fouled by Scotti. Free kick in a central position 25 yards from goal. Muntari is over the ball...
Muntari leaves it for Gyan, who curls the ball horribly wide. Goal kick.
Diego Forlan's shot goes in.

Kingson saves from Suarez at his near post! Fucile passes to Lodeiro, who plays a delightful ball to Suarez. He aims a shot at the near post, but Kingson is equal to it. The latest Uruguay corner is cleared.
Poor clearance from Victorino. It falls to Gyan, he takes one touch to control, and then unleashes a half volley. Good idea, but Muslera is equal to it.
Appiah on for Inkoom
Forlan and Suarez celebrate.
With either of these two teams have the courage to push for a winner?
Cavani to be replaced by Abreu
Sarpei booked for shirt pulling. Forlan over another Uruguay set piece...
His driven ball reaches Suarez, whose glancing header is well saved by Kingson. The corner from Forlan causes mayhem, as eventually Ghana earn a curious free kick.
ESPN Stats & Info: Uruguay have scored three goals after the 76th minute in this World Cup while Ghana have scored two.
Uruguay's substitutions suggest Tabarez is trying to win this before extra time.
Paintsil's long throw is headed behind by Victorino. Muntari with a corner.

Muslera is unconvincing from the corner, but Uruguay clear and break. Somehow Maxi Pareira finds himself on the edge of the Ghana box, and with better options available he elects to shoot. Wrong decision.
From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Suarez got very greedy there as he had options. This has turned into a great game. No need to fear the ides of Japan versus Paraguay.
Uruguay looking the more dangerous side as we approach full time. Can they find a winner?
Fucile is the latest Uruguay full back to venture forward. His cross is poor and goes straight out of play.
The game is drifting towards extra time. Can anyone be the hero?
Muntari off for Adiyiah
Ghana have a throw deep in Uruguay territory. Paintsil is winding up a long one...
Late corner for Ghana...
THREE MINUTES ADDED TIME
Is there a late twist in the tale? Throw in Uruguay...
Ghana clear and look to break. Appiah finds Boateng, his cross is too long and Uruguay have the ball.
FULL TIME This one is going to extra time.

From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Extra-time and both teams have given plenty already. Ghana may perhaps be tired after their efforts against USA with many of their team having been nursed back to fitness. Neither team are sat down, which used to be such a feature of extra-times of days gone by.
KICK OFF Uruguay start extra time.
Ghana have been here before, in the last round against USA. Will that experience help them?
Stat Attack:: Neither team have ever been in a penalty shootout at the World Cup.
Ghana win the first corner of extra time...
Abreu with a diving header behind. Desperate stuff from Uruguay.
Mensah is carded for what seems to be dissent.
The corner is headed clear. The set pieces, barring Forlan's excellent strike, have been disappointing.
Asamoah lets fly from fully 40 yards. He's no Muntari though, his effort is well wide.
Mensah down after seemingly clashing with Abreu. There didn't seem to be much in that. He's on his feet and the game continues.
Arevalo almost gifts Ghana the lead! Gyan catches the midfielder in possession in the Uruguay box. Scotti gets across and manages to eventually halt Gyan.
Gyan is down and being put on a stretcher, this is worrying for Ghana.
Gyan is back on his feet, thankfully for Ghana. They're going to need him if they're going to find another goal.
The game is far more cagey in extra time. Understandably, neither team wants to lose it at this stage.
Maxi Pereira gets forward again, Ghana concede yet another corner.
Abreu hits the deck looking for a penalty. Pereira's cross is aimed at the big man, Paintsil trips him before the ball arrives. A lucky escape for Ghana.
First real contribution from Lodeiro. He finds himself behind the Ghana defence, his low cross is deflected goalwards. Kingson reacts smartly to keep the ball out.
A Forlan burst down the left results in ANOTHER Uruguay corner.
ONE MINUTE ADDED TIME
Kingson punches the ball clear very well indeed.
HALF TIME IN EXTRA TIME
KICK OFF Ghana start the second half of extra time.
A clever ball played over the top for Gyan. It's just too long for him, though, and drifts out of play.
The pace of the game has tailed off dramatically. Who can blame them? Both teams have given their all in this contest.
Free kick to Ghana, Lodeiro commiting a foul.
Scotti with a strong defensive header. Ghana start again, Appiah with a dangerous cross. Gyan gets his head on the ball, but can't guide it on target.
From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Both teams with opportunities and neither are not going forward but there does seems an air of inevitability about the penalties.
Ghana almost unlock Uruguay! Appiah arriving at the back post. He should've headed the ball first time, instead he chests it down and the chance has gone.
At the other end, Forlan has a sight of goal. A deep cross arrives on his left foot. He lashes a shot just over Kingson's bar.

So close for Ghana! Gyan show great strength to hold off Scotti. He twists clear, attempts to shoot, Scotti manages to get a foot on his shot and almost diverts the ball into his own goal! Chaos for a moment there!

From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Unbelievable passage of play there. It seemed destined for an own-goal DVD near you. Scotti can thank Perreira that Danny Dyer will not be scoffing at him this Christmas.
Gyan again showing tremendous desire, getting to the byline and making Fucile concede a corner...
Abreu serves his purpose in the penalty box. Heading out for a throw.
Fucile flicks a Paintsil throw in towards his own goal. Boateng gets his header away and he is so close to winning it for Ghana!
Muslera scrambles the ball away! A Boateng crosses spirals towards the Uruguay goal, the keeper makes a desperate save!
Last minute free kick for Ghana. Can they win it...
Penalty Uruguay
Luis Suarez sent off for clearing the ball off the line with his hand.Penalty!
Gyan's penalty hits the bar! What a climax in Johannesburg!
FULL TIME What drama! Penalties!
Will Asamoah Gyan step forward to take Ghana's first penalty? What must he be feeling now?

From John Brewin in Johannesburg: Wow, just wow. Unbelievable. One of the most amazing sequences I have ever seen in football. Gyan now has to take a penalty. Suarez was in tears but is now celebrating wildly.
Who can step forward and claim their place in the semi-final?
So here we go...
Diego Forlan to take the first penalty...
Uruguay 1-0 Ghana Forlan calmly to the left of Kingson.
Gyan steps up once again...
Uruguay 1-1 Ghana A great penalty by Gyan! Right in the top corner, what courage!
Victorino for Uruguay...
Uruguay 2-1 Ghana A fantastic penalty. High to the right of Kingson.
Stephen Appiah next up...
Uruguay 2-2 Ghana Another calm penalty.
Scotti for Uruguay...
Uruguay 3-2 Ghana Straight down the middle!
John Mensah next for Ghana...
Uruguay 3-2 Ghana Muslera saves low to his left!
Maxi Pereira to give Uruguay the advantage...
Uruguay 3-2 Ghana Pereira hits it way over! An awful penalty!
Adiyiah steps forward for Ghana...
Uruguay 3-2 Ghana Muslera saves again! Low to his left again. Match point Uruguay!
Abreu can win the shoot out for Uruguay...
Uruguay 4-2 Ghana What a penalty! Abreu chips it calmly down the middle! Uruguay into the semi-finals!
What a night in Johannesburg. Has the World Cup over known drama like we have seen in the last half an hour?!
You feel for Ghana, and poor, poor Asamoah Gyan. He deserves better from his tournament.

From John Brewin in Johannesburg: After a sequence of panicked penalties I could never forget, that was the coollest I have ever seen. Abreu did an Antonin Panenka and a continent mourns. Drama that knows few bounds.
Abreu with possibly the calmest penalty in the history of football. Hats off to you, sir!
Read our match report on this epic contest here.
